evidence: Deprecated compatibility surfaces are still active in primary feature code: `src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` is marked deprecated but `src/pages/Home/components/panes/CreativePartnerPane.tsx` still imports `useIsMobile` from it.

Runtime/integration boundary is blurred by a long-lived compatibility shim: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exports deprecated `supabase` proxy at module scope and lazily initializes on property access.

Cross-feature orchestration relies on global event names instead of explicit contracts: `useSettingsModal` listens to `openSettings` and `useMediaGalleryHandlers` listens to `selectShotForAddition` through shared event infrastructure.
UI utility boundary is inconsistent: `button.tsx` and `MediaTypeFilter.tsx` consume `cn` via `@/shared/lib/utils`, while `tooltip.tsx` and `label.tsx` consume `@/shared/components/ui/cn` directly.

`useButtonGroupProps` rebuilds the same `topRight`/`bottomLeft`/`bottomRight` object shape field-by-field and tracks a long dependency list, adding a pass-through abstraction layer with no behavioral translation (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts`).

`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` composes multiple large param bags (`panel`, `shotWorkflow`, `actions`, `buttonGroupProps`) and forwards many fields unchanged into downstream layout/workflow plumbing, increasing indirection cost (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx`).

`useInlineEditState` and `useImageEditValue` rely on an oversized interface (`UseImageEditValueParams`) and repeated `Pick<...>`+mapping builders that mostly copy hook outputs into a new object graph, creating ceremony-heavy abstraction rather than reducing complexity (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts`, `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts`).

`tooltip.tsx` re-exports `TouchableTooltip` while `touchableTooltip.tsx` imports from `tooltip.tsx`, creating a circular wrapper dependency for a convenience export (`src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/touchableTooltip.tsx`).

Multiple compatibility shims remain active across shared surfaces: `src/shared/components/BatchDropZone.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/utils/download.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useBatchImageLoading.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useClickRipple.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx`, `src/shared/lib/NetworkStatusManager.ts`, `src/shared/lib/aspectRatios.ts`, `src/shared/lib/autoplayMonitor.ts`, `src/shared/lib/clientThumbnailGenerator.ts`, and `src/shared/lib/deepEqual.ts`.
`src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` still imports `ConfirmDialog` from deprecated shim path `@/shared/components/ConfirmDialog` instead of canonical dialogs package.
`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` still exports deprecated `supabase` compatibility proxy with lazy fallback behavior and a future removal target, so legacy and new access patterns coexist.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` keeps deprecated structure-video fields in task params/interfaces, preserving legacy payload shape alongside new `structure_guidance` model.
`src/features/prompt-editor/components/BulkEditControls.tsx` is an explicit migration-compat re-export, indicating ownership move is still incomplete.

holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles shows `src/shared/hooks/` has 95 production files (well above the >10 mixed-concern overload signal), and assigned files `useApiTokens.ts`, `useAsyncOperation.ts`, and `useAutoSaveSettings.ts` represent different concerns (account API token domain, async utility, settings persistence orchestration) inside the same flat folder.

holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles shows `src/shared/components/` has 80 files with mixed responsibilities; assigned `ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx` and `AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx` are at root level despite existing specialized component subfolders (`generation-controls`, app-specific error boundary locations), indicating weak directory boundaries.

The assigned files have parallel copies in alternate package paths (`src/shared/hooks/async/useAsyncOperation.ts`, `src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts`, `src/shared/components/generation-controls/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx`, and app error-boundary path), which creates ambiguous ownership and split navigation paths.

Silent catch blocks are present in runtime paths: `src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts:11-13` ignores storage failures, and `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/StrokeOverlay.tsx:166` and `:231` swallow pointer-capture errors with empty catches.

Error shapes are inconsistent across adjacent flows: `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts:115-116` throws raw `variantError` while neighboring branches throw `new Error(...)`; `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:45-48` rethrows unknown errors without normalization.

Logging strategy differs by module boundary: `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:515` and `:524` use `console.error` plus `handleError(...)`, while edge functions such as `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:156-161` and `supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts:160` use ad-hoc console/logger paths.

findings.summary: Deprecated mobile-hook compatibility surface remains in active UI paths, extending boundary blur and migration debt.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` declares itself deprecated and intended only as compatibility surface.
`src/pages/Home/components/panes/CreativePartnerPane.tsx` imports `useIsMobile` from `@/shared/hooks/useMobile`, keeping callers on the legacy entrypoint.
findings.suggestion: Migrate active imports to `@/shared/hooks/mobile/*` modules directly (starting with `CreativePartnerPane`) and add an import rule to block new imports from `@/shared/hooks/useMobile` except approved legacy files.

findings.summary: Supabase module keeps a deprecated proxy export that hides initialization order and preserves a broad compatibility shim.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exports `supabase = createSupabaseCompatibilityProxy()` at module scope with deprecation metadata and lazy runtime fallback behavior.
`getSupabaseClient()` catches runtime-access-before-bootstrap and falls back to initialization, indicating tolerated boot-order violations.
This path also routes through shared `handleError`, coupling runtime client access behavior to global error infrastructure.
findings.suggestion: Finish removal of `supabase` compatibility export by migrating remaining imports to `getSupabaseClient()` plus explicit bootstrap; then fail fast (without fallback initialize) when runtime is uninitialized outside sanctioned bootstrap contexts.

findings.summary: Feature behavior is coordinated through global app events, creating hidden coupling and weak dependency direction.
evidence: `useSettingsModal` listens for `openSettings` via `useAppEventListener` instead of explicit prop/context orchestration.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers` listens for `selectShotForAddition` through the same global event mechanism.
These string-keyed cross-feature interactions make dependencies implicit and harder to trace during refactors.
findings.suggestion: Constrain global events to a narrow boundary (for truly app-level concerns), and move feature-specific flows to typed context/state APIs with explicit ownership and dependency direction.

findings.summary: The `cn` helper is consumed through multiple architectural paths, signaling duplicated/unclear shared utility boundary.
evidence: `button.tsx` and `MediaTypeFilter.tsx` import `cn` from `@/shared/lib/utils`.
`tooltip.tsx` and `label.tsx` import `cn` from `@/shared/components/ui/cn`.
`src/shared/lib/utils.ts` re-exports `cn`, creating two active entrypoints for the same concern.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one canonical `cn` module for UI code and remove or deprecate the alternate re-export path; enforce with lint/ts-path rules to prevent future split usage.

findings.summary: Button-group hook is a pure pass-through wrapper that adds dependency and maintenance overhead without adding behavior.
evidence: `useButtonGroupProps` copies every input field directly into a returned object and uses a dependency array mirroring those same fields.
`LightboxLayout` consumes `props.buttonGroupProps.*` directly, so the intermediate wrapper does not validate, normalize, or reduce coupling.
findings.suggestion: Remove `useButtonGroupProps` and pass grouped props directly from the source model, or replace it with a small normalizer that performs actual translation/defaulting so the abstraction has clear leverage.

findings.summary: Video lightbox render-model builds and forwards broad option bags across layers, obscuring ownership and inflating interface surface.
evidence: `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` takes five high-entropy inputs (`props`, `modeModel`, `env`, `sharedState`, `editModel`) and forwards many values unchanged into `useLightboxWorkflowProps` via nested `panel/shotWorkflow/actions/buttonGroupProps` objects.

The resulting `layoutProps` is then handed to layout rendering, creating a long wrapper chain (`VideoLightbox` -> render model -> layout props -> `LightboxLayout`) where much of the data remains structurally identical.
findings.suggestion: Split render-model construction into narrower contracts per concern (navigation, panel, workflow actions), and pass typed domain objects rather than full cross-cutting bags to reduce pass-through coupling.

findings.summary: Inline edit state uses an oversized parameter interface and mapping builders that mostly restate existing hook outputs.
evidence: `UseImageEditValueParams` defines a very large all-in-one contract spanning mode state, canvas, transform, form values, and generation status.

`useInlineEditState` builds this through three `Pick<ImageEditValueParams, ...>` helper assemblers and then `useImageEditValue` reassembles it into `ImageEditState` via four more builder functions, with mostly field-for-field forwarding.
findings.suggestion: Replace the monolithic `UseImageEditValueParams` with composable sub-interfaces (e.g., mode/canvas/transform/generation slices) and allow downstream consumers to read slice objects directly instead of remapping every field.

findings.summary: UI tooltip convenience re-export creates a circular dependency between base tooltip and touchable wrapper modules.
evidence: `tooltip.tsx` exports `TouchableTooltip` from `touchableTooltip.tsx`.
`touchableTooltip.tsx` imports `Tooltip`, `TooltipContent`, `TooltipProvider`, and `TooltipTrigger` from `tooltip.tsx`, creating a cycle rooted in a re-export abstraction.
findings.suggestion: Break the cycle by moving shared tooltip primitives to a third module (or importing primitive package directly in `touchableTooltip.tsx`) and keep re-export barrels acyclic.

findings.summary: Migration shims are spread across many shared entrypoints, indicating dual-path imports are still normalized rather than contained.
evidence: Each file is a deprecated compatibility re-export with guidance to import from a new canonical path.
The pattern repeats across components, hooks, and lib modules, increasing chance of new legacy imports and prolonging coexistence.
findings.suggestion: Create a migration burn-down pass that replaces imports to canonical targets repo-wide, then enforce via lint rule (`no-restricted-imports`) against these shim paths and remove shims batch-by-batch.

findings.summary: Active code still consumes a deprecated compatibility path, proving migration is not just historical residue.
evidence: `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` imports `{ ConfirmDialog }` from `@/shared/components/ConfirmDialog`.
`src/shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx` is explicitly marked deprecated and forwards to `@/shared/components/dialogs/ConfirmDialog`.
findings.suggestion: Update `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` to import from `@/shared/components/dialogs/ConfirmDialog` directly, then scan for remaining imports of the deprecated shim and remove them before shim deletion.

findings.summary: Supabase client module still publishes both new runtime API and deprecated proxy API, keeping migration debt in a high-traffic integration boundary.
evidence: `client.ts` exposes `initializeSupabase()`/`getSupabaseClient()` and also deprecated `export const supabase = createSupabaseCompatibilityProxy()`.
Deprecated export has explicit future removal metadata, but remains active and includes lazy fallback path and warnings, preserving old behavior.
findings.suggestion: Instrument and report remaining `supabase` named-import callsites, migrate them to `getSupabaseClient()` + bootstrap initialization, then remove the proxy export by the stated target date.

findings.summary: Legacy travel-between-images payload fields remain part of exported task parameter types, sustaining old schema usage in active code paths.
evidence: `legacyStructureVideo.ts` exports interfaces containing many `@deprecated` fields (`structure_video_*`, `uni3c_*`, `use_uni3c`) alongside new guidance fields.
Deprecated fields are kept in first-class exported types rather than isolated to a narrow adapter layer, which allows legacy shape to continue propagating.
findings.suggestion: Move deprecated fields behind a dedicated normalization adapter type (input-only), keep domain/task output types on the new schema only, and reject legacy keys at new callsites after a migration window.

findings.summary: Critical hooks with different ownership are colocated in an overloaded flat `shared/hooks` package.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/` has 95 files in `directory_profiles`, exceeding the >10 mixed-concern threshold by a large margin.

`useApiTokens.ts` is account/billing specific and tied to Supabase PAT flows, while `useAsyncOperation.ts` is generic async infrastructure and `useAutoSaveSettings.ts` is settings persistence orchestration.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create target folders `src/features/account/hooks/`, `src/shared/hooks/async/`, and `src/shared/settings/hooks/`; (2) move `useApiTokens.ts` to `src/features/account/hooks/useApiTokens.ts`, then `useAsyncOperation.ts` to `src/shared/hooks/async/useAsyncOperation.ts`, then `useAutoSaveSettings.ts` to `src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts`; (3) update imports with `rg -n "@/shared/hooks/(useApiTokens|useAsyncOperation|useAutoSaveSettings)" src` and replace to new paths; (4) validate with `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` and focused tests `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAsyncOperation.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts`.

findings.summary: Assigned hook/test files coexist with duplicate module trees, creating unclear canonical locations.
evidence: There are parallel paths for the same hooks (`src/shared/hooks/async/useAsyncOperation.ts` and `src/shared/settings/hooks/useAutoSaveSettings.ts`) while assigned legacy-path tests still live under `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/`.
This creates two navigation/ownership paths for the same behavior and increases import drift risk.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) pick canonical packages (`src/shared/hooks/async/` for async operation, `src/shared/settings/hooks/` for auto-save settings); (2) move the assigned tests to mirror canonical folders (`src/shared/hooks/async/__tests__/...`, `src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/...`); (3) leave temporary compatibility re-exports at old paths for one release cycle; (4) run `rg -n "useAsyncOperation|useAutoSaveSettings" src` to migrate imports, then validate with `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/async/__tests__/useAsyncOperation.test.ts src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/useAutoSaveSettings.test.ts`.

findings.summary: Root-level component files are placed outside stronger domain folders despite clear domain affinity.
evidence: `src/shared/components/` has 80 files and mixes many unrelated domains; this exceeds the flat-directory cohesion signal.

`ActiveLoRAsDisplay` is LoRA/generation-specific and already has a parallel `generation-controls` location in the repo, while `AppErrorBoundary` tests exist under shared root despite app-level error-boundary structure elsewhere.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) set target folders: `src/shared/components/generation-controls/` for `ActiveLoRAsDisplay*` and `src/app/components/error/__tests__/` for app error-boundary test files; (2) move component/test files in order: `ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx`, `ActiveLoRAsDisplay.test.tsx`, then `AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx`; (3) update imports via `rg -n "shared/components/(ActiveLoRAsDisplay|AppErrorBoundary)" src`; (4) validate with `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` and targeted tests `pnpm vitest src/shared/components/generation-controls/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.test.tsx src/app/components/error/__tests__/AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx`.

findings.summary: High-fan-in compatibility shims have removal dates but no enforceable retirement gate, so old/new contracts can persist indefinitely.
evidence: README only defines an explicit gate for `@/types/shots` shim removal (`npm run check:shots-shim-usage`).
`client.ts` still exports deprecated `supabase` compatibility proxy and lazy fallback path with a dated removal target but no codified check in docs.
`handleError.ts` still exports deprecated alias symbols with removal target metadata and no listed quality gate.
findings.suggestion: Add contract-governance checks to `quality:check` for all dated shims: (1) enforce zero imports of `supabase` named export from `@/integrations/supabase/client`, (2) enforce zero imports/usages of `reportRuntimeError` and `RuntimeErrorOptions`, (3) fail CI when a dated shim has passed its removal date. Implement with a small script using `rg` patterns and wire it into `npm run quality:check`; validate via `rg "import\s*\{\s*supabase\s*\}" src`, `rg "reportRuntimeError|RuntimeErrorOptions" src`, and `npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: A generic `shared/lib/utils` barrel in an overloaded directory blurs ownership and pulls UI primitives through a catch-all package.
evidence: `src/shared/lib/` has 79 files in structure profile (mixed concerns threshold exceeded).
`utils.ts` exports unrelated concerns (`cn` UI class helper and string formatting helpers) under a generic name, encouraging broad dependency spread.
`button.tsx` (UI primitive) imports `cn` from `@/shared/lib/utils`, reinforcing cross-boundary dependency on a catch-all module.
findings.suggestion: Run a staged reorg: Stage 1 target folders: keep `cn` in UI scope (`src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts` or `src/shared/lib/uiClassNames.ts` as single source), move string helpers to `src/shared/lib/string/index.ts`; Stage 2 move order: migrate UI imports from `@/shared/lib/utils` to UI-scoped `cn` path first, then migrate non-UI string helper imports to `@/shared/lib/string`, then deprecate and remove `src/shared/lib/utils.ts`; Stage 3 import-update/validation commands: `rg "@/shared/lib/utils" -n src`, apply codemod/replacements, then run `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test -- src/shared/components/ui/button.test.tsx`, and `npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Multiple catches intentionally suppress errors without structured reporting, creating blind spots in failure diagnostics.
evidence: `useDiscordSignIn` swallows `localStorage.setItem` failures (`catch { /* intentionally ignored */ }`) at `src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts:11-13`.
`StrokeOverlay` swallows pointer capture/release exceptions with empty catches at `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/StrokeOverlay.tsx:166` and `:231`.
findings.suggestion: Replace empty catches with a non-user-facing reporting path (for example `handleError(error, { context: ..., showToast: false, logData: ... })`) and keep suppression only where explicitly justified with a comment about fallback behavior.

findings.summary: Similar async mutation/query flows throw heterogeneous error types (raw objects vs `Error`), weakening predictable error handling contracts.
evidence: `useGenerationMutations` throws raw `variantError` (`throw variantError`) at `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts:115-116`, but other branches throw `new Error(...)` (e.g. `:48`, `:98`, `:134`).
`useTasks` catches and rethrows unknown values (`throw error`) at `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:45-48`, preserving non-Error values and bypassing normalization.
Task creators commonly follow `handleError(...); throw error;` pattern (e.g. `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:441-443`), so mixed upstream throw types propagate through shared handlers.
findings.suggestion: Enforce an `Error`-only boundary before rethrow (convert unknown/raw SDK errors via a normalizer) and standardize rethrow helpers so mutation/query `onError` consumers always receive a consistent error shape.

findings.summary: Cross-cutting error logging uses different channels and duplication patterns, making traces inconsistent across frontend tasks and edge functions.
evidence: In `imageGeneration`, dev-only `console.error` is used in addition to `handleError(...)` (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:515`, `:524`, `:526`), creating dual logging paths.

`complete_task/generation` logs via `console.error` and `logger?.error` before rethrow (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:156-161`), while other edge code returns JSON errors directly after console logging (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts:160-161`).
findings.suggestion: Define one logging policy per layer (frontend runtime vs edge runtime), then route all caught failures through that policy (single helper/wrapper) to avoid duplicate logs and inconsistent metadata fields.
